headline: Trump orders US military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian small boats in Strait of Hormuz
summary: US President Trump has ordered military forces to shoot and kill Iranian small boats that are choking the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in the ongoing conflict.

headline: Iran seizes vessel in Strait of Hormuz, defying US naval superiority
summary: Iran flaunted its tightened grip over the Strait of Hormuz with a video of commandos storming a cargo ship after peace talks collapsed between Washington and Tehran.

headline: Iran's top diplomat set to travel to Pakistan for renewed talks to end war with US
summary: Iran's foreign minister is expected in Islamabad for talks, raising hopes for renewed negotiations to end the war between the US and Iran.

headline: Pentagon email floats suspending Spain from NATO over Iran war rift
summary: An internal Pentagon email outlines options to punish NATO allies, including suspending Spain from the alliance, over perceived lack of support for US operations in the Iran war.

headline: UK insists Falklands sovereignty unchanged after US 'review' report
summary: Downing Street asserts Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK after an internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of reviewing the territory's status in retaliation for the UK not joining the Iran war.

headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is 'in excellent physical condition' after having a malignant tumor removed.

headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

headline: Iran's exiled crown prince Reza Pahlavi splattered with red liquid in Berlin
summary: Reza Pahlavi, the exiled crown prince of Iran, was targeted with red liquid in an incident in Berlin.

headline: Progressive Bulgaria secures majority in parliament
summary: With 87% of the vote counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

headline: Poisoning suspected in deaths of 18 wolves in Italian national park
summary: After the carcasses of 10 wolves were found last week, another eight have been found in recent days in an Italian national park.
